tasks.description: Upgrade the context engine by introducing structured Context Layers: Session Context, Working Memory, Tool Context, Skill Context, and Belief State.
tasks.details:
Context Layers to implement:
1. Session Context: What the user is doing right now (task, intent, last commands).
2. Working Memory: Short-term facts the agent inferred or extracted.
3. Tool Context: Results, errors, and metadata from recent tool calls.
4. Skill Context: Which skills were used, their confidence, and arbitration scores.
5. Belief State: Bayesian reasoning output (probabilities, uncertainty).

Then feed a summarized, structured version of these into the model each turn.

subtasks.title: Define structs/models for the 5 Context Layers
Implement working memory and short-term fact extraction
Implement context summarization logic to prevent token bloat
Integrate summarized Context Layers into the final LLM prompt

tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for each context layer's state management.
2. Integration tests ensuring summarized context is properly injected into the prompt.
3. Verify token usage remains efficient with the new summarization.
tasks.title: Implement Context Engine 2.0

tasks.description: Leverage the Bayesian layer to help with prompt shaping, adapting the prompt based on uncertainty, vagueness, repetition, and tool failures.
tasks.details:
Implement prompt shaping behaviors driven by the Bayesian engine:
- If uncertainty is high -> ask a clarification.
- If the user is vague -> propose interpretations.
- If the user repeats themselves -> infer intent drift.
- If a tool failed -> rewrite the prompt to avoid the failure mode.

subtasks.title: Implement high-uncertainty detection and clarification prompting
Add vagueness detection and interpretation proposals
Implement repetition detection to infer intent drift
Add dynamic prompt rewriting for tool failure recovery

tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests mocking high uncertainty to verify clarification prompts are generated.
2. Unit tests for intent drift detection on repeated inputs.
3. Integration tests verifying tool failure triggers prompt rewriting.
tasks.title: Context-Aware Prompt Refinement

tasks.description: When conversation history approaches the model token limit, automatically summarize the oldest messages with the AI, persist them to the session archive on disk, replace them with a compact notice in the active context, and allow retrieval via /recall or a recall_context tool.
tasks.details:
## Problem
The current trimming strategy (count-based + token-budget) simply drops old messages when context fills up. This permanently loses potentially important conversation history. Users working on long coding sessions frequently lose earlier decisions, file edits, and reasoning chains.

## Goal
Implement a four-stage context lifecycle that replaces blind dropping with intelligent summarization:

1. DETECT — when estimated prompt tokens exceed `compression_threshold` (default 75%) of `max_context_tokens`, trigger compression.
2. SUMMARIZE — take the oldest `compression_chunk_ratio` (default 40%) of messages and make a fast API call to produce a bullet-point summary + key_facts list.
3. ARCHIVE — persist the full raw messages + summary to disk at `~/.grok/sessions/{session_id}/archives/chunk_NNN.json` and update an `index.json`.
4. REPLACE — remove the archived messages from `session.messages` and insert a compact system-role archive-notice message (~50 tokens) so the model knows the history exists and how to recall it.

## Archive Notice Format (injected into active context)
```
[Context Archive #N | {timestamp}]
Summary: {2-3 sentence AI summary of what was discussed/done}.
Key facts: {bullet list}.
Say "recall archive N" or type `/recall N` to restore this context.
```

## Storage Layout
```
~/.grok/sessions/{session_id}/
  episode.json          (existing — end-of-session metadata)
  transcript.json       (existing — full raw transcript)
  archives/
    index.json          (list of chunk IDs + token counts)
    chunk_001.json      (raw messages + AI summary)
    chunk_002.json
    ...
```

## New Rust Modules
- `src/memory/context_archive.rs` — `ContextChunk`, `ContextArchive` structs + atomic disk I/O.
- `src/memory/context_compressor.rs` — `ContextCompressor` that calls the Grok API to summarize a message batch.

## New Config Fields (AcpConfig in src/config/mod.rs)
```toml
# Master switch — disable to revert to old drop-only behaviour.
auto_compress = true
# Fraction of max_context_tokens at which compression fires (0.0–1.0).
compression_threshold = 0.75
# Fraction of current non-system messages compressed per event (0.0–1.0).
compression_chunk_ratio = 0.40
```

## Integration Point
Layer 4 is added to `handle_chat_completion` in `src/acp/mod.rs`, after the existing three layers (per-message truncation, count trim, token-budget trim).

## New Slash Commands (src/acp/slash_commands.rs)
- `/recall N` — retrieve chunk N back into active context as a system message.
- `/archives` — list all archived chunks for this session (ID, timestamp, token count, one-line summary).

## New Tool (src/tools/ or src/acp/tools.rs)
- `recall_context { chunk_id }` — allows the AI itself to pull an archived chunk back in when it detects it needs earlier information. Returns the summary + key_facts; the full raw messages are injected as a system message.

tasks.description: Update all model references, capabilities, and context-management logic to fully leverage grok-4.3's 1,000,000-token context window while keeping backward compatibility with grok-3 (256 k) and grok-2 (131 k).
tasks.details:
## Background
grok-4.3 is now the default model and exposes a 1,048,576-token context window — roughly 4× the grok-3 window.  The current code has several hardcoded or config-defaulted values that are tuned for grok-3 (256 k) and must be updated.

## Changes Required

### 1. src/acp/mod.rs — create_capabilities()
- Set `max_context_length: 1_048_576` (up from 131_072).
- Add `"grok-4.3"` as the first entry in `models` vec; keep grok-3, grok-3-mini, grok-2-vision-1212, grok-2 as fallbacks.
- Add feature flags: `"1m_context"`, `"vision"` (grok-4.3 is multimodal).

### 2. src/config/mod.rs — AcpConfig
- Add new field `grok4_max_context_tokens: usize` (default: 950_000).  This is the soft budget for grok-4.x models — leaves 50 k headroom for response + tool definitions.
- Rename/update the doc comment on `max_context_tokens` to clarify it applies to grok-3 and older.
- Add `fn default_grok4_max_context_tokens() -> usize { 950_000 }`.
- Update `AcpConfig::default()` to include the new field.

### 3. src/acp/mod.rs — handle_chat_completion (Layer 3 token-budget trim)
- Replace the single `max_ctx_tokens` lookup with model-aware selection:
```rust
let max_ctx_tokens = model_context_budget(
    &session.config.model,
    self.config.acp.max_context_tokens,
    self.config.acp.grok4_max_context_tokens,
);
```
- Add free function `fn model_context_budget(model: &str, grok3_budget: usize, grok4_budget: usize) -> usize` that returns grok4_budget when `model.starts_with("grok-4")`, else grok3_budget.
- Apply the same model-aware budget in Layer 4 (compression threshold calculation).

### 4. src/acp/mod.rs — SessionConfig::default()
- Keep model as `"grok-4.3"`.
- Raise `max_tokens` (output) to 16_384 — grok-4.3 supports higher output token limits.

### 5. src/config/mod.rs — Config defaults
- Update `default_max_tokens()` from 256_000 to 16_384 (this is the output-token budget; 256 k was erroneously set to match the input window).
- Update comment on `default_max_context_tokens()` to clarify 220_000 is the grok-3 budget.

### 6. config.example.toml + .grok/config.toml
- Add `grok4_max_context_tokens = 950000` under `[acp]`.
- Update comments to describe the model-selection logic.

### 7. CONFIGURATION.md
- Add a "Model Context Budgets" section documenting grok-4.3 vs grok-3 limits.

### 8. CHANGELOG.md
- Add entry for v0.2.0 noting grok-4.3 default and 1 M context support.

tasks.description: Add first-class support for grok-4.3 thinking/reasoning modes via the xAI API's reasoning_effort parameter.  Users can switch between Off / Low / High reasoning at runtime; thinking tokens are surfaced in the terminal UI and optionally persisted with session history.
tasks.details:
## Background
The xAI API exposes a `reasoning_effort` field on chat completion requests for models that support extended thinking (grok-3-mini, grok-4.x).  Valid values:
- `"none"` — standard response, no internal reasoning trace.
- `"low"` — light reasoning; faster and cheaper.
- `"high"` — deep reasoning; highest quality, slower, higher token cost.
The response may include a `thinking` or `reasoning_content` field in `choices[0].message` containing the model's internal chain-of-thought.

## Changes Required

### 1. src/config/mod.rs
- Add `ThinkingMode` enum:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
pub enum ThinkingMode {
    #[default]
    Off,    // reasoning_effort = "none" (not sent for non-reasoning models)
    Low,    // reasoning_effort = "low"
    High,   // reasoning_effort = "high"
}
```
- Add `thinking_mode: ThinkingMode` field to `AcpConfig` with `#[serde(default)]` and `default_thinking_mode` fn returning `ThinkingMode::Off`.

### 2. src/acp/mod.rs — SessionConfig
- Add `pub thinking_mode: ThinkingMode` field to `SessionConfig`.
- Update `SessionConfig::default()` to set `thinking_mode: ThinkingMode::Off`.

### 3. src/grok_client_ext.rs — GrokClient::chat_completion_with_history
- Add `reasoning_effort: Option<&str>` parameter (or `thinking_mode: Option<ThinkingMode>`).
- If `Some` and not `ThinkingMode::Off`, append `"reasoning_effort": "low"|"high"` to the JSON request body.
- Since grok_api v0.1.7's builder may not expose this field, inject it by either:
  (a) Adding a `.reasoning_effort(val)` builder method to the local grok_api crate (`../grok_crate/grok_api/src/client.rs`), or
  (b) Adding an `extra_body` field to the builder that merges a `serde_json::Map` into the request body before serialization.
- Preferred: option (a) — add `reasoning_effort: Option<String>` to the request builder in grok_api.

### 4. src/grok_client_ext.rs — MessageWithFinishReason + response parsing
- Extend `MessageWithFinishReason` with `pub thinking_content: Option<String>`.
- In `convert_response_to_message_with_finish_reason`: extract `choices[0].message.reasoning_content` or `choices[0].message.thinking` if present and store in `thinking_content`.
- Forward `thinking_content` back through `handle_chat_completion` → display layer.

### 5. src/acp/mod.rs — handle_chat_completion
- Pass `session.config.thinking_mode` (converted to `Option<&str>`) when calling `chat_completion_with_history`.
- After receiving the response, if `thinking_content` is Some, prepend it to the assistant message as a fenced block:
```
<details><summary>🧠 Thinking…</summary>

{thinking_content}

</details>
```
- Emit a `debug!()` log with token count of thinking content (len / 4).

### 6. src/acp/slash_commands.rs — /think command
- Add `SlashCommand::Think { mode: ThinkingMode }`.
- Parse `/think` → list current mode.
- Parse `/think off` → ThinkingMode::Off.
- Parse `/think low` → ThinkingMode::Low.
- Parse `/think high` → ThinkingMode::High.
- Call a new `GrokAcpAgent::set_thinking_mode(session_id, mode)` method.
- Return a `BuiltinResult::Handled(msg)` describing the change.

### 7. src/acp/mod.rs — set_thinking_mode()
- Add `pub async fn set_thinking_mode(&self, session_id: &SessionId, mode: ThinkingMode) -> Result<()>`.

### 8. src/main.rs / CLI chat command
- Add `--thinking <off|low|high>` flag to the `chat` subcommand.
- Pass it through `SessionConfig` when starting a chat session.

### 9. Config files + docs
- Add `thinking_mode = "off"` to `[acp]` in `config.example.toml` with a comment.
- Document in `CONFIGURATION.md`.
- Add CHANGELOG entry.

## Network / Starlink Considerations
Thinking mode requests take significantly longer (especially `high`).  Ensure:
- `timeout_secs` is respected (already configured for long operations).
- The existing Starlink retry loop covers thinking-mode requests without special changes (they're the same HTTP call, just longer).

tasks.description: Replace grok-cli's hand-rolled ACP JSON-RPC implementation with the official agent-client-protocol crate (v0.11+). Three phases: A) research & code audit, B) schema-type swap then connection-layer rewrite, C) adopt all new protocol features the crate unlocks (session resume, elicitation, session fork, MCP-over-ACP bridging).
tasks.details:
## Why migrate?
The official crate (https://crates.io/crates/agent-client-protocol v0.11+) is maintained by the Zed team, powers Zed's own agent integration natively, and has >1.2 M downloads in the last 90 days. grok-cli currently hand-rolls every ACP wire-format type in src/acp/protocol.rs and the entire JSON-RPC dispatch loop in src/cli/commands/acp.rs (~1 800 lines). The multi-word slash-command name bug fixed in May 2026 is a textbook example of what the crate's strongly-typed schema would have caught at compile time. Migrating means every future ACP protocol update becomes a one-line version bump instead of manual archaeology.

## Phase A — Research & Audit (111.1)
Read the crate source + docs.rs, and compare every type in src/acp/protocol.rs against agent_client_protocol::schema::*. Categorise each type as REPLACE / EXTEND / KEEP. Produce Doc/acp-migration-map.md.

## Phase B — Migration (111.2 – 111.4)
111.2  Schema-types swap: add the crate as a real dependency (default-features = false), replace REPLACE types in protocol.rs with re-exports / type aliases, keep grok-specific structs (BayesEngine, ThinkingMode, Goal, etc.).
111.3  Connection-layer rewrite: replace the raw BufReader/BufWriter loop in run_acp_session with the crate's Builder + Agent trait. Map each existing handler (handle_initialize, handle_session_new, handle_session_prompt, etc.) to the corresponding agent callback. Keep all grok custom session logic untouched (Bayesian engine, context archives, goals, thinking mode).
111.4  Slash-command advertisement: replace the hand-rolled AvailableCommand struct with agent_client_protocol::schema::AvailableCommand — verify the crate enforces single-word names at the type level so the class of bug fixed in task 110 can never recur.

## Phase C — New Protocol Features (111.5 – 111.8)
111.5  Session resume (unstable_session_resume): persist full session state to .grok/sessions/<id>.json on close; reload it on session/load. Replaces the current stub that always returns null history.
111.6  Elicitation (unstable_elicitation): replace the ad-hoc PermissionBridge with the official elicitation RPC so Zed / Gemini CLI show structured Allow / Allow Always / Deny dialogs.
111.7  Session fork (unstable_session_fork): clone session state (messages + config + Bayes engine + goal) into a new session ID when a client sends session/fork — enables Zed's branch-conversation feature.
111.8  MCP-over-ACP bridging (agent_client_protocol::mcp_server): expose grok-cli's 32 LLM-callable tools as a standard MCP server endpoint over the ACP transport so any MCP client can discover and invoke them.

tasks.description: Design and implement a Text-Graph Synergy RAG engine for grok-cli using a hybrid tree-sitter + syn parser to build a semantic-entity project graph (structs, enums, traits, impls, functions, modules), coupled with embeddings and hybrid retrieval (BM25 + vectors) to drive graph-aware, retrieval-aware context compression and LLM prompting.
tasks.details:
## Background
Current grok-cli context handling includes context discovery and AI-powered summarization/archiving, but it is linear and text-only. The next step is a Text-Graph Synergy RAG (TGS-RAG) engine that couples a semantic project graph with embeddings and hybrid retrieval. The goal is to retrieve minimal, highly relevant context for each request by traversing a semantic entity graph (structs, enums, traits, impls, functions, modules) and combining it with vector search and BM25.

This epic uses a hybrid parsing approach: tree-sitter for fast, incremental file/function boundaries and syn for deep semantic extraction. The resulting graph will power graph-expanded retrieval, reranking, and Context Compression 2.0.

## Scope
- Build a semantic-entity project graph for Rust (and later, language-agnostic hooks).
- Integrate embeddings + BM25 hybrid retrieval.
- Implement graph-expanded retrieval and reranking.
- Implement Context Compression 2.0 (retrieval-aware pruning + summarization).
- Integrate with ACP sessions, Session DNA, and existing context-discovery pipeline.
- Add configuration, docs, and tests.

## Out of Scope
- Non-Rust language-specific parsers (future tasks).
- Full MCP-based external knowledge sources (future tasks).
